name: Jellied Turkey-Vegetable Salad (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 42 ounces cream of celery soup, condensed (4 cans)
2 1/2 pounds cooked turkey, finely diced (8 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, leaving enough extra for folding over the top. Use one pan per six servings or one-fourth of the recipe.
2. Do not line pans if food will not be frozen.
3. Soften gelatin in cold water.
4. Heat a cup of soup and add gelatin. Stir until gelatin is dissolved.
5. Add turkey, vegetables, and salad dressing. Mix gently.
6. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each pan.
7. Chill until firm.
To Serve Without Freezing:
- Serve on crisp salad greens.
To Freeze:
1. Remove freezer wrap.
2. Place food in a baking pan.
3. Seal with freezer tape.
4. Label with name, date frozen, and last usable date (about 6 months).
5. Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from pans.
To Thaw Frozen Salad:
- Remove freezer wrap.
- Place food in a baking pan.
- Thaw in refrigerator for about 24 hours.
- Serve on crisp salad greens.
Serving Ideas: Crisp salad greens
NOTES: This recipe serves 24 portions (approximately 2/3 cup each). Instructions are provided to divide the prepared food into four parts of six servings each. One part can be fully cooked and served at preparation time, while the remaining parts can be frozen.
